summary.summary: Adds a defensive minimum glucose floor in BaseGlucoseStorage: a new Config.minimumGlucose = 39 and a private clampToMinimum(_:) helper that maps BloodGlucose entries to ensure glucose and sgv are at least 39 (and emits a debug(.deviceManager, ...) message with the raw value and timestamp). (diff:0fa6abdab965) storeGlucose(_) and backfillGlucose(_) run their inputs through clampToMinimum before filtering/persistence, and storeCGMState is called with the clamped array. (diff:0fa6abdab965) addManualGlucose is intentionally left unchanged so user-entered fingerstick values are not rewritten. (pr:nightscout/Trio#1159) The change includes unit tests that verify sub-39 CGM readings are clamped on storeGlucose and backfillGlucose. (file:7fb3f94207a3)
summary.userFacingImpact: Conservative summary: CGM-sourced glucose readings below 39 mg/dL will be clamped to 39 before being filtered or persisted, which may change how very-low CGM readings influence determinations or stored history when upstream plugins (e.g. LibreTransmitter) forward values below the vendor floor. (pr:gordolio/Trio#1159) User-entered manual/fingerstick glucose values are not modified by this change. (diff:0fa6abdab965) The PR also logs the raw low CGM reading and timestamp for diagnostics rather than silently discarding it. (diff:0fa6abdab965) If you need a precise assessment of how this affects closed-loop dosing in all deployment configurations, human review is recommended. (pr:nightscout/Trio#1159)
